title: China’s Questionable Tech Strategy
excerpt: Keeping out the world’s best tools promises to stymie innovation.
content_html:
Late last year, President Trump announced that Washington would loosen controls on chip exports to China, permitting Nvidia to sell its H200 AI chip to approved Chinese firms. The president’s decision raised the alarm among lawmakers that he was sacrificing American security and the country’s edge in AI for a trade deal with China’s leader, Xi Jinping. But any concern that Chinese companies would be gobbling up precious Nvidia chips was soon squelched by Beijing’s own ban on orders of the H200. Officials have recently allowed some companies to use these chips, but have taken pains to limit their supply. Beijing would prefer for Chinese firms to use local chips, even if they aren’t as good.

To expand the country’s global power and protect its economy from foreign threats, China’s leaders have worked to disconnect its tech sector from the rest of the world. They want Chinese companies to market Chinese technology created in Chinese labs and built from Chinese supply chains. The strategy appears to be working, given the many Chinese electric vehicles humming down European streets and the AI models from Chinese firms such as DeepSeek and Z.ai alarming Silicon Valley. Yet China’s growing isolation threatens to slow innovation within the country by keeping out the world’s best tech. In truly global technological industries, such as AI, China’s defensive insularity may well hold the country back.

China’s rise and leadership in various sectors can actually be attributed to its previous openness to foreign ingenuity. In the 1980s, when the country began modernizing its economy by engaging in global trade, China’s outdated industries voraciously absorbed foreign technology. Beijing’s strategic planners then introduced a slew of supportive policies to nurture and protect new industries, which helped China become a leader in sectors such as telecommunications and high-speed rail.

The rise of digital media compromised the value of openness, moving Beijing to erect the Great Firewall to shield citizens from the flow of undesirable ideas and information. Global internet platforms and services such as Facebook, Google, and X got shut out, and Chinese alternatives rose in their place. Companies that adhered to Beijing’s strict censorship rules, such as the search-engine provider Baidu and the WeChat operator Tencent, became titans; they had 1.1 billion customers largely to themselves. But as relative latecomers to the internet revolution, domestically focused and dogged by Beijing’s information controls, these firms never truly rivaled Meta and Alphabet in global appeal. The standout exception, TikTok, founded by the Chinese tech firm ByteDance, can’t operate in China.

Eric Schmidt and Selina Xu: China is building the future

Anyone traveling to China will soon discover the day-to-day oddities of this digital landscape. Chinese people message one another on WeChat, not WhatsApp or Signal. Instead of scrolling Instagram or TikTok, locals post on Weibo, RedNote, and Douyin.

Xi sharpened China’s inward turn in tech about a decade ago, as geopolitical tensions flared between the United States and China. Sensing that China’s dependence on foreign technology was a national-security liability, and that developing this technology was necessary for the country’s economic ascent, he moved swiftly to expand domestic research and manufacturing and to encourage more self-sufficiency in semiconductor production and supply chains. Xi’s government has spent hundreds of billions of dollars to support domestic chips, electric vehicles, AI, and other emerging areas to rival the most advanced Western industries.

In some sectors, Xi’s program is succeeding. China has built an almost entirely Chinese EV supply chain, including the batteries that propel them and the software that runs them. But no single country, not even China, can create the best of everything. China’s efforts to build a fully Chinese supply chain for computer chips, for example—partly spurred by President Biden’s 2022 export controls on advanced American AI chips and chipmaking equipment to China—has proved uniquely hard. The process of making semiconductors is immensely complex and dauntingly global, involving supply chains that span numerous countries. Re-creating that chain in one place is “essentially impossible,” Scott Kennedy, a senior adviser at the Center for Strategic and International Studies who has studied the Chinese tech industry, told me. Even attempting it is “cost-prohibitive and self-defeating.”

China will continue to pursue its own chip industry anyway. Yet this emphasis on self-sufficiency has surely come at a cost. China is expending massive resources creating its own versions of what the rest of the world already has. And though the Chinese chip industry is making progress, it’s still playing catch-up with the West. China’s AI firms have had to operate without the best chips, and this disadvantage will likely last if Beijing insists that they use Chinese alternatives. Sharing technology across borders “is the way a lot of innovation happens,” particularly in the semiconductor industry, Paul Triolo, a specialist in Chinese technology at the consulting firm Albright Stonebridge Group, told me. Now that there are “two bifurcated stacks”—in China and the U.S.—“some of the innovation won’t happen,” he added.

Like their internet-platform predecessors, China’s AI firms are emerging in a heavily censored and protected domestic market. The mandate that Chinese companies and consumers use AI models developed by DeepSeek, Alibaba, Moonshot, and other Chinese firms, not OpenAI’s ChatGPT or Anthropic’s Claude, means that local users are being deprived of the world’s top AI at a time when the technology is influencing economies and societies across the globe. In April, Beijing’s regulators took the unusual step of ordering Meta to reverse its $2 billion acquisition of the Singapore-based AI firm Manus, founded in China and known for creating deeply researched reports, on national-security grounds. The message to Chinese tech entrepreneurs was clear: Cooperating with foreigners is unacceptable.

Michael Schuman: China is abusing AI

Beijing is threatening to hold back Chinese tech in other ways too. China’s AI models are growing in popularity around the world, in part because they are mostly open-source, which means users anywhere can adopt and adapt them at no cost. Yet Chinese regulators, wary of foreign competitors exploiting Chinese expertise, are reportedly discussing new restrictions on foreign access to the most advanced Chinese AI models, which promises to curb their rise and appeal.

In a sign that Chinese companies recognize that they are still trailing the advances of their American rivals, American AI firms are accusing their Chinese competitors of “distilling,” or harvesting superior U.S. models to train their own. In a letter sent to two U.S. senators last month and reviewed by The Atlantic, Anthropic alleged that the Chinese e-commerce company Alibaba, which operates a model called Qwen, “executed the largest known distillation attack” on Claude, using about 25,000 fraudulent accounts to illicitly extract some of its “most valuable capabilities.” (An Alibaba spokesperson declined to comment.) Anthropic has responded to these assaults by strengthening Claude’s defenses and hunting down the Chinese accounts that improperly access the model, thereby reinforcing China’s technological isolation and potentially compromising Chinese efforts to keep pace with resource-rich American rivals.

Perhaps China’s technology sector will further spin off in its own direction, creating products that are somehow separate from but equal to those in the United States. Triolo reckons that the AI universe could split in two, with China and the U.S. at the center of dueling worlds and little connectivity between them.

What appears just as likely is that China, in creating an impenetrable fortress around its own tech sector and limiting the country’s access to the best tech from elsewhere, is dooming itself to a distant second place.

sentences.en: Late last year, President Trump announced that Washington would loosen controls on chip exports to China, permitting Nvidia to sell its H200 AI chip to approved Chinese firms.
sentences.zh: 去年年底，特朗普总统宣布华盛顿将放松对中国芯片出口的控制，允许英伟达向获得批准的中国公司出售其 H200 人工智能芯片。

sentences.en: The president’s decision raised the alarm among lawmakers that he was sacrificing American security and the country’s edge in AI for a trade deal with China’s leader, Xi Jinping.
sentences.zh: 总统的决定引起了立法者的警惕，他们认为他为了与中国领导人习近平达成贸易协议而牺牲了美国的安全和美国在人工智能领域的优势。

sentences.en: But any concern that Chinese companies would be gobbling up precious Nvidia chips was soon squelched by Beijing’s own ban on orders of the H200.
sentences.zh: 但有关中国公司将吞噬宝贵的英伟达芯片的担忧很快就被北京方面禁止 H200 订单的担忧所消除。

sentences.en: Officials have recently allowed some companies to use these chips, but have taken pains to limit their supply.
sentences.zh: 官员们最近允许一些公司使用这些芯片，但煞费苦心地限制其供应。

sentences.en: Beijing would prefer for Chinese firms to use local chips, even if they aren’t as good.
sentences.zh: 北京方面更希望中国企业使用本土芯片，即使它们的性能没有那么好。

blocks.html_en: To expand the country’s global power and protect its economy from foreign threats, China’s leaders have worked to disconnect its tech sector from the rest of the world. They want Chinese companies to market Chinese technology created in Chinese labs and built from Chinese supply chains. The strategy appears to be working, given the many Chinese electric vehicles humming down European streets and the AI models from Chinese firms such as DeepSeek and Z.ai alarming Silicon Valley. Yet China’s growing isolation threatens to slow innovation within the country by keeping out the world’s best tech. In truly global technological industries, such as AI, China’s defensive insularity may well hold the country back.

sentences.en: To expand the country’s global power and protect its economy from foreign threats, China’s leaders have worked to disconnect its tech sector from the rest of the world.
sentences.zh: 为了扩大国家的全球影响力并保护其经济免受外国威胁，中国领导人一直致力于将其科技行业与世界其他地区脱钩。

sentences.en: They want Chinese companies to market Chinese technology created in Chinese labs and built from Chinese supply chains.
sentences.zh: 他们希望中国公司推销在中国实验室创造并由中国供应链构建的中国技术。

sentences.en: The strategy appears to be working, given the many Chinese electric vehicles humming down European streets and the AI models from Chinese firms such as DeepSeek and Z.ai alarming Silicon Valley.
sentences.zh: 鉴于许多中国电动汽车在欧洲街道上嗡嗡作响，以及 DeepSeek 和 Z.ai 等中国公司的人工智能模型让硅谷感到震惊，这一战略似乎正在发挥作用。

sentences.en: Yet China’s growing isolation threatens to slow innovation within the country by keeping out the world’s best tech.
sentences.zh: 然而，中国的日益孤立可能会阻碍世界上最好的技术，从而减缓该国的创新速度。

sentences.en: In truly global technological industries, such as AI, China’s defensive insularity may well hold the country back.
sentences.zh: 在人工智能等真正的全球科技产业中，中国的防御性孤立态度很可能会阻碍该国的发展。

blocks.html_en: China’s rise and leadership in various sectors can actually be attributed to its previous openness to foreign ingenuity. In the 1980s, when the country began modernizing its economy by engaging in global trade, China’s outdated industries voraciously absorbed foreign technology. Beijing’s strategic planners then introduced a slew of supportive policies to nurture and protect new industries, which helped China become a leader in sectors such as telecommunications and high-speed rail.

sentences.en: China’s rise and leadership in various sectors can actually be attributed to its previous openness to foreign ingenuity.
sentences.zh: 中国的崛起和在各个领域的领导地位实际上可以归因于其之前对外国创造力的开放。

sentences.en: In the 1980s, when the country began modernizing its economy by engaging in global trade, China’s outdated industries voraciously absorbed foreign technology.
sentences.zh: 20世纪80年代，当中国开始通过参与全球贸易实现经济现代化时，中国的落后工业大量吸收外国技术。

sentences.en: Beijing’s strategic planners then introduced a slew of supportive policies to nurture and protect new industries, which helped China become a leader in sectors such as telecommunications and high-speed rail.
sentences.zh: 随后，北京的战略规划者推出了一系列扶持政策来培育和保护新兴产业，帮助中国成为电信和高铁等行业的领导者。

blocks.html_en: The rise of digital media compromised the value of openness, moving Beijing to erect the Great Firewall to shield citizens from the flow of undesirable ideas and information. Global internet platforms and services such as Facebook, Google, and X got shut out, and Chinese alternatives rose in their place. Companies that adhered to Beijing’s strict censorship rules, such as the search-engine provider Baidu and the WeChat operator Tencent, became titans; they had 1.1 billion customers largely to themselves. But as relative latecomers to the internet revolution, domestically focused and dogged by Beijing’s information controls, these firms never truly rivaled Meta and Alphabet in global appeal. The standout exception, TikTok, founded by the Chinese tech firm ByteDance, can’t operate in China.

sentences.en: The rise of digital media compromised the value of openness, moving Beijing to erect the Great Firewall to shield citizens from the flow of undesirable ideas and information.
sentences.zh: 数字媒体的兴起损害了开放的价值，促使北京建立防火墙以保护公民免受不良思想和信息的影响。

sentences.en: Global internet platforms and services such as Facebook, Google, and X got shut out, and Chinese alternatives rose in their place.
sentences.zh: Facebook、Google 和 X 等全球互联网平台和服务被拒之门外，中国的替代品取而代之。

sentences.en: Companies that adhered to Beijing’s strict censorship rules, such as the search-engine provider Baidu and the WeChat operator Tencent, became titans; they had 1.1 billion customers largely to themselves.
sentences.zh: 遵守北京严格审查规则的公司，例如搜索引擎提供商百度和微信运营商腾讯，成为了巨头；他们拥有 11 亿客户，其中大部分是他们自己的。

sentences.en: But as relative latecomers to the internet revolution, domestically focused and dogged by Beijing’s information controls, these firms never truly rivaled Meta and Alphabet in global appeal.
sentences.zh: 但作为互联网革命的相对较晚者，这些公司专注于国内并受到北京信息控制的困扰，在全球吸引力方面从未真正与 Meta 和 Alphabet 相媲美。

sentences.en: The standout exception, TikTok, founded by the Chinese tech firm ByteDance, can’t operate in China.
sentences.zh: 一个突出的例外是，由中国科技公司字节跳动创立的 TikTok 无法在中国运营。

blocks.html_en: Eric Schmidt and Selina Xu: China is building the future

sentences.en: Eric Schmidt and Selina Xu: China is building the future
sentences.zh: 埃里克·施密特和徐琳娜：中国正在建设未来

blocks.html_en: Anyone traveling to China will soon discover the day-to-day oddities of this digital landscape. Chinese people message one another on WeChat, not WhatsApp or Signal. Instead of scrolling Instagram or TikTok, locals post on Weibo, RedNote, and Douyin.

sentences.zh: 任何到中国旅游的人很快就会发现这个数字景观的日常怪异之处。

sentences.en: Chinese people message one another on WeChat, not WhatsApp or Signal.
sentences.zh: 中国人通过微信互相发送信息，而不是 WhatsApp 或 Signal。

sentences.en: Instead of scrolling Instagram or TikTok, locals post on Weibo, RedNote, and Douyin.
sentences.zh: 当地人不再使用 Instagram 或 TikTok，而是在微博、RedNote 和抖音上发帖。

sentences.en: Xi sharpened China’s inward turn in tech about a decade ago, as geopolitical tensions flared between the United States and China.
sentences.zh: 大约十年前，随着中美地缘政治紧张局势的加剧，习近平加强了中国科技领域的内向转向。

sentences.en: Sensing that China’s dependence on foreign technology was a national-security liability, and that developing this technology was necessary for the country’s economic ascent, he moved swiftly to expand domestic research and manufacturing and to encourage more self-sufficiency in semiconductor production and supply chains.
sentences.zh: 他意识到中国对外国技术的依赖是国家安全的负担，而且开发这项技术对于国家经济的崛起是必要的，因此他迅速采取行动扩大国内研究和制造，并鼓励半导体生产和供应链实现更多自给自足。

sentences.en: Xi’s government has spent hundreds of billions of dollars to support domestic chips, electric vehicles, AI, and other emerging areas to rival the most advanced Western industries.
sentences.zh: 习近平政府已花费数千亿美元支持国内芯片、电动汽车、人工智能和其他新兴领域，以与西方最先进的产业相抗衡。

sentences.en: In some sectors, Xi’s program is succeeding.
sentences.zh: 在某些领域，习近平的计划正在取得成功。

sentences.en: China has built an almost entirely Chinese EV supply chain, including the batteries that propel them and the software that runs them.
sentences.zh: 中国已经建立了几乎完全中国的电动汽车供应链，包括驱动它们的电池和运行它们的软件。

sentences.en: But no single country, not even China, can create the best of everything.
sentences.zh: 但没有一个国家，甚至中国，能够创造出最好的一切。

sentences.en: China’s efforts to build a fully Chinese supply chain for computer chips, for example—partly spurred by President Biden’s 2022 export controls on advanced American AI chips and chipmaking equipment to China—has proved uniquely hard.
sentences.zh: 例如，中国建立完全中国化的计算机芯片供应链的努力（部分原因是拜登总统 2022 年对中国先进的人工智能芯片和芯片制造设备实施出口管制）已被证明是极其困难的。

sentences.en: The process of making semiconductors is immensely complex and dauntingly global, involving supply chains that span numerous countries.
sentences.zh: 半导体的制造过程极其复杂，而且是全球性的，涉及跨越多个国家的供应链。

sentences.en: Re-creating that chain in one place is “essentially impossible,” Scott Kennedy, a senior adviser at the Center for Strategic and International Studies who has studied the Chinese tech industry, told me.
sentences.zh: 研究中国科技行业的战略与国际研究中心高级顾问斯科特·肯尼迪告诉我，在一个地方重新创建这条链条“基本上是不可能的”。

sentences.en: Even attempting it is “cost-prohibitive and self-defeating.”
sentences.zh: 即使尝试它也是“成本高昂且弄巧成拙”。

Now that there are “two bifurcated stacks”—in China and the U.S.—“some of the innovation won’t happen,” he added.

sentences.en: China will continue to pursue its own chip industry anyway.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，中国将继续发展自己的芯片产业。

sentences.en: Yet this emphasis on self-sufficiency has surely come at a cost.
sentences.zh: 然而，这种对自给自足的强调肯定是有代价的。

sentences.en: China is expending massive resources creating its own versions of what the rest of the world already has.
sentences.zh: 中国正在花费大量资源来创建自己的世界其他国家已有的版本。

sentences.en: And though the Chinese chip industry is making progress, it’s still playing catch-up with the West.
sentences.zh: 尽管中国芯片行业正在取得进步，但仍在追赶西方。

sentences.en: China’s AI firms have had to operate without the best chips, and this disadvantage will likely last if Beijing insists that they use Chinese alternatives.
sentences.zh: 中国的人工智能公司不得不在没有最好的芯片的情况下运营，如果北京坚持使用中国的替代品，这种劣势可能会持续下去。

sentences.en: Sharing technology across borders “is the way a lot of innovation happens,” particularly in the semiconductor industry, Paul Triolo, a specialist in Chinese technology at the consulting firm Albright Stonebridge Group, told me.
sentences.zh: 咨询公司奥尔布赖特石桥集团 (Albright Stonebridge Group) 的中国技术专家保罗·特里奥洛 (Paul Triolo) 告诉我，跨境共享技术“是许多创新发生的方式”，尤其是在半导体行业。

sentences.en: Now that there are “two bifurcated stacks”—in China and the U.S.—“some of the innovation won’t happen,” he added.
sentences.zh: 现在在中国和美国存在“两个分歧的堆栈”，“一些创新将不会发生，”他补充道。

blocks.html_en: Like their internet-platform predecessors, China’s AI firms are emerging in a heavily censored and protected domestic market. The mandate that Chinese companies and consumers use AI models developed by DeepSeek, Alibaba, Moonshot, and other Chinese firms, not OpenAI’s ChatGPT or Anthropic’s Claude, means that local users are being deprived of the world’s top AI at a time when the technology is influencing economies and societies across the globe. In April, Beijing’s regulators took the unusual step of ordering Meta to reverse its $2 billion acquisition of the Singapore-based AI firm Manus, founded in China and known for creating deeply researched reports, on national-security grounds. The message to Chinese tech entrepreneurs was clear: Cooperating with foreigners is unacceptable.

sentences.en: Like their internet-platform predecessors, China’s AI firms are emerging in a heavily censored and protected domestic market.
sentences.zh: 与他们的互联网平台前辈一样，中国的人工智能公司正在受到严格审查和保护的国内市场中崛起。

sentences.en: The mandate that Chinese companies and consumers use AI models developed by DeepSeek, Alibaba, Moonshot, and other Chinese firms, not OpenAI’s ChatGPT or Anthropic’s Claude, means that local users are being deprived of the world’s top AI at a time when the technology is influencing economies and societies across the globe.
sentences.zh: 要求中国企业和消费者使用 DeepSeek、阿里巴巴、Moonshot 和其他中国公司开发的人工智能模型，而不是 OpenAI 的 ChatGPT 或 Anthropic 的 Claude，这意味着在该技术影响全球经济和社会的同时，本地用户却被剥夺了使用世界顶级人工智能的机会。

sentences.en: In April, Beijing’s regulators took the unusual step of ordering Meta to reverse its $2 billion acquisition of the Singapore-based AI firm Manus, founded in China and known for creating deeply researched reports, on national-security grounds.
sentences.zh: 今年 4 月，北京监管机构以国家安全为由，采取了不同寻常的举措，命令 Meta 撤销对新加坡人工智能公司 Manus 的 20 亿美元收购。Manus 成立于中国，以撰写深入研究的报告而闻名。

sentences.en: The message to Chinese tech entrepreneurs was clear: Cooperating with foreigners is unacceptable.
sentences.zh: 向中国科技企业家传达的信息很明确：与外国人合作是不可接受的。

blocks.html_en: Michael Schuman: China is abusing AI

sentences.en: Michael Schuman: China is abusing AI
sentences.zh: 迈克尔·舒曼：中国正在滥用人工智能

blocks.html_en: Beijing is threatening to hold back Chinese tech in other ways too. China’s AI models are growing in popularity around the world, in part because they are mostly open-source, which means users anywhere can adopt and adapt them at no cost. Yet Chinese regulators, wary of foreign competitors exploiting Chinese expertise, are reportedly discussing new restrictions on foreign access to the most advanced Chinese AI models, which promises to curb their rise and appeal.

sentences.en: Beijing is threatening to hold back Chinese tech in other ways too.
sentences.zh: 北京还威胁要以其他方式遏制中国科技。

sentences.en: China’s AI models are growing in popularity around the world, in part because they are mostly open-source, which means users anywhere can adopt and adapt them at no cost.
sentences.zh: 中国的人工智能模型在世界各地越来越受欢迎，部分原因是它们大多是开源的，这意味着任何地方的用户都可以免费采用和适应它们。

sentences.en: Yet Chinese regulators, wary of foreign competitors exploiting Chinese expertise, are reportedly discussing new restrictions on foreign access to the most advanced Chinese AI models, which promises to curb their rise and appeal.
sentences.zh: 然而，据报道，中国监管机构担心外国竞争对手利用中国的专业知识，正在讨论对外国企业获取最先进的中国人工智能模型实施新的限制，这有望遏制其崛起和吸引力。

blocks.html_en: In a sign that Chinese companies recognize that they are still trailing the advances of their American rivals, American AI firms are accusing their Chinese competitors of “distilling,” or harvesting superior U.S. models to train their own. In a letter sent to two U.S. senators last month and reviewed by The Atlantic, Anthropic alleged that the Chinese e-commerce company Alibaba, which operates a model called Qwen, “executed the largest known distillation attack” on Claude, using about 25,000 fraudulent accounts to illicitly extract some of its “most valuable capabilities.” (An Alibaba spokesperson declined to comment.) Anthropic has responded to these assaults by strengthening Claude’s defenses and hunting down the Chinese accounts that improperly access the model, thereby reinforcing China’s technological isolation and potentially compromising Chinese efforts to keep pace with resource-rich American rivals.

sentences.en: In a sign that Chinese companies recognize that they are still trailing the advances of their American rivals, American AI firms are accusing their Chinese competitors of “distilling,” or harvesting superior U.S. models to train their own.
sentences.zh: 美国人工智能公司指责中国竞争对手“提炼”或收获美国的优秀模型来训练自己的模型，这表明中国公司认识到自己仍然落后于美国竞争对手。

sentences.en: In a letter sent to two U.S. senators last month and reviewed by The Atlantic, Anthropic alleged that the Chinese e-commerce company Alibaba, which operates a model called Qwen, “executed the largest known distillation attack” on Claude, using about 25,000 fraudulent accounts to illicitly extract some of its “most valuable capabilities.”
sentences.zh: 《大西洋月刊》在上个月发给两名美国参议员的一封信中，Anthropic 声称，运营名为 Qwen 的模式的中国电子商务公司阿里巴巴对克劳德“实施了已知最大规模的蒸馏攻击”，利用约 25,000 个欺诈账户非法提取其一些“最有价值的能力”。

sentences.en: (An Alibaba spokesperson declined to comment.)
sentences.zh: （阿里巴巴发言人拒绝置评。）

sentences.en: Anthropic has responded to these assaults by strengthening Claude’s defenses and hunting down the Chinese accounts that improperly access the model, thereby reinforcing China’s technological isolation and potentially compromising Chinese efforts to keep pace with resource-rich American rivals.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 通过加强 Claude 的防御并追查以不当方式访问该模型的中国账户来应对这些攻击，从而加强了中国的技术孤立，并可能损害中国与资源丰富的美国竞争对手保持同步的努力。

blocks.html_en: Perhaps China’s technology sector will further spin off in its own direction, creating products that are somehow separate from but equal to those in the United States. Triolo reckons that the AI universe could split in two, with China and the U.S. at the center of dueling worlds and little connectivity between them.

sentences.en: Perhaps China’s technology sector will further spin off in its own direction, creating products that are somehow separate from but equal to those in the United States.
sentences.zh: 也许中国的科技行业将进一步朝着自己的方向发展，创造出与美国产品有所不同但又平等的产品。

sentences.en: Triolo reckons that the AI universe could split in two, with China and the U.S. at the center of dueling worlds and little connectivity between them.
sentences.zh: Triolo 认为人工智能世界可能会一分为二，中国和美国处于竞争世界的中心，而且两国之间几乎没有联系。

blocks.html_en: What appears just as likely is that China, in creating an impenetrable fortress around its own tech sector and limiting the country’s access to the best tech from elsewhere, is dooming itself to a distant second place.

sentences.en: What appears just as likely is that China, in creating an impenetrable fortress around its own tech sector and limiting the country’s access to the best tech from elsewhere, is dooming itself to a distant second place.
sentences.zh: 同样有可能的是，中国在自己的科技行业周围建立了一座坚不可摧的堡垒，并限制该国从其他地方获得最好的技术，这注定会让自己远远落后于第二位。

content_html_zh:
去年年底，特朗普总统宣布华盛顿将放松对中国芯片出口的控制，允许英伟达向获得批准的中国公司出售其 H200 人工智能芯片。 总统的决定引起了立法者的警惕，他们认为他为了与中国领导人习近平达成贸易协议而牺牲了美国的安全和美国在人工智能领域的优势。 但有关中国公司将吞噬宝贵的英伟达芯片的担忧很快就被北京方面禁止 H200 订单的担忧所消除。 官员们最近允许一些公司使用这些芯片，但煞费苦心地限制其供应。 北京方面更希望中国企业使用本土芯片，即使它们的性能没有那么好。

为了扩大国家的全球影响力并保护其经济免受外国威胁，中国领导人一直致力于将其科技行业与世界其他地区脱钩。 他们希望中国公司推销在中国实验室创造并由中国供应链构建的中国技术。 鉴于许多中国电动汽车在欧洲街道上嗡嗡作响，以及 DeepSeek 和 Z.ai 等中国公司的人工智能模型让硅谷感到震惊，这一战略似乎正在发挥作用。 然而，中国的日益孤立可能会阻碍世界上最好的技术，从而减缓该国的创新速度。 在人工智能等真正的全球科技产业中，中国的防御性孤立态度很可能会阻碍该国的发展。

中国的崛起和在各个领域的领导地位实际上可以归因于其之前对外国创造力的开放。 20世纪80年代，当中国开始通过参与全球贸易实现经济现代化时，中国的落后工业大量吸收外国技术。 随后，北京的战略规划者推出了一系列扶持政策来培育和保护新兴产业，帮助中国成为电信和高铁等行业的领导者。

数字媒体的兴起损害了开放的价值，促使北京建立防火墙以保护公民免受不良思想和信息的影响。 Facebook、Google 和 X 等全球互联网平台和服务被拒之门外，中国的替代品取而代之。 遵守北京严格审查规则的公司，例如搜索引擎提供商百度和微信运营商腾讯，成为了巨头；他们拥有 11 亿客户，其中大部分是他们自己的。 但作为互联网革命的相对较晚者，这些公司专注于国内并受到北京信息控制的困扰，在全球吸引力方面从未真正与 Meta 和 Alphabet 相媲美。 一个突出的例外是，由中国科技公司字节跳动创立的 TikTok 无法在中国运营。

埃里克·施密特和徐琳娜：中国正在建设未来

任何到中国旅游的人很快就会发现这个数字景观的日常怪异之处。 中国人通过微信互相发送信息，而不是 WhatsApp 或 Signal。 当地人不再使用 Instagram 或 TikTok，而是在微博、RedNote 和抖音上发帖。

大约十年前，随着中美地缘政治紧张局势的加剧，习近平加强了中国科技领域的内向转向。 他意识到中国对外国技术的依赖是国家安全的负担，而且开发这项技术对于国家经济的崛起是必要的，因此他迅速采取行动扩大国内研究和制造，并鼓励半导体生产和供应链实现更多自给自足。 习近平政府已花费数千亿美元支持国内芯片、电动汽车、人工智能和其他新兴领域，以与西方最先进的产业相抗衡。

在某些领域，习近平的计划正在取得成功。 中国已经建立了几乎完全中国的电动汽车供应链，包括驱动它们的电池和运行它们的软件。 但没有一个国家，甚至中国，能够创造出最好的一切。 例如，中国建立完全中国化的计算机芯片供应链的努力（部分原因是拜登总统 2022 年对中国先进的人工智能芯片和芯片制造设备实施出口管制）已被证明是极其困难的。 半导体的制造过程极其复杂，而且是全球性的，涉及跨越多个国家的供应链。 研究中国科技行业的战略与国际研究中心高级顾问斯科特·肯尼迪告诉我，在一个地方重新创建这条链条“基本上是不可能的”。 即使尝试它也是“成本高昂且弄巧成拙”。

无论如何，中国将继续发展自己的芯片产业。 然而，这种对自给自足的强调肯定是有代价的。 中国正在花费大量资源来创建自己的世界其他国家已有的版本。 尽管中国芯片行业正在取得进步，但仍在追赶西方。 中国的人工智能公司不得不在没有最好的芯片的情况下运营，如果北京坚持使用中国的替代品，这种劣势可能会持续下去。 咨询公司奥尔布赖特石桥集团 (Albright Stonebridge Group) 的中国技术专家保罗·特里奥洛 (Paul Triolo) 告诉我，跨境共享技术“是许多创新发生的方式”，尤其是在半导体行业。 现在在中国和美国存在“两个分歧的堆栈”，“一些创新将不会发生，”他补充道。

与他们的互联网平台前辈一样，中国的人工智能公司正在受到严格审查和保护的国内市场中崛起。 要求中国企业和消费者使用 DeepSeek、阿里巴巴、Moonshot 和其他中国公司开发的人工智能模型，而不是 OpenAI 的 ChatGPT 或 Anthropic 的 Claude，这意味着在该技术影响全球经济和社会的同时，本地用户却被剥夺了使用世界顶级人工智能的机会。 今年 4 月，北京监管机构以国家安全为由，采取了不同寻常的举措，命令 Meta 撤销对新加坡人工智能公司 Manus 的 20 亿美元收购。Manus 成立于中国，以撰写深入研究的报告而闻名。 向中国科技企业家传达的信息很明确：与外国人合作是不可接受的。

迈克尔·舒曼：中国正在滥用人工智能

北京还威胁要以其他方式遏制中国科技。 中国的人工智能模型在世界各地越来越受欢迎，部分原因是它们大多是开源的，这意味着任何地方的用户都可以免费采用和适应它们。 然而，据报道，中国监管机构担心外国竞争对手利用中国的专业知识，正在讨论对外国企业获取最先进的中国人工智能模型实施新的限制，这有望遏制其崛起和吸引力。

美国人工智能公司指责中国竞争对手“提炼”或收获美国的优秀模型来训练自己的模型，这表明中国公司认识到自己仍然落后于美国竞争对手。 《大西洋月刊》在上个月发给两名美国参议员的一封信中，Anthropic 声称，运营名为 Qwen 的模式的中国电子商务公司阿里巴巴对克劳德“实施了已知最大规模的蒸馏攻击”，利用约 25,000 个欺诈账户非法提取其一些“最有价值的能力”。 （阿里巴巴发言人拒绝置评。） Anthropic 通过加强 Claude 的防御并追查以不当方式访问该模型的中国账户来应对这些攻击，从而加强了中国的技术孤立，并可能损害中国与资源丰富的美国竞争对手保持同步的努力。

也许中国的科技行业将进一步朝着自己的方向发展，创造出与美国产品有所不同但又平等的产品。 Triolo 认为人工智能世界可能会一分为二，中国和美国处于竞争世界的中心，而且两国之间几乎没有联系。

同样有可能的是，中国在自己的科技行业周围建立了一座坚不可摧的堡垒，并限制该国从其他地方获得最好的技术，这注定会让自己远远落后于第二位。
